milestoneFrameworkJson.deliverable_statement: The system can ingest the full Zendesk knowledge base and product catalogue into a searchable index, with a validated sample of 50 queries showing correct source retrieval from both data sources.

The chatbot is live on internal test environment, answering support agent queries with a measured accuracy of 85% or higher on a 200-query benchmark set, with visible source citations on every answer.

The system is deployed to production, achieving under 3-second response time for 95% of queries, with automatic escalation of low-confidence answers to human agents and a weekly accuracy report dashboard.
A 30-day production performance report showing sustained accuracy above 85%, daily query volume handling of 2000+, and customer churn metrics from support interactions compared to the prior quarter.

sdlc_notices: The existing 71% accuracy baseline is self-reported and has no standardised measurement methodology; the first benchmark set must be agreed before launch to avoid disputes about improvement.
Zendesk knowledge base and product catalogue data must be accessible via stable REST APIs; if API rate limits or authentication barriers exist, data ingestion timelines will shift.
Low-confidence answers escalate to humans, but the escalation threshold must be calibrated carefully—too aggressive and agents are overwhelmed, too lenient and bad answers still reach customers.
Weekly accuracy review process requires a human feedback loop; the team must commit reviewer time or the quality improvement cycle stalls.
Redis caching will be used to meet the 3-second latency target for frequently asked queries; cache invalidation strategy needs to be defined when Zendesk or product data updates.
artifactAJson.business_intent: Replace the current low-accuracy customer support chatbot with a retrieval-augmented system that sources answers directly from Zendesk knowledge base and product catalogue. The goal is to increase answer accuracy from 71% to above 85%, reduce customer churn caused by wrong answers, and provide source citations so agents and customers can trust the responses.

stack_tags: Vector DB (e.g. pgvector, Weaviate, or Qdrant)
Embedding model (e.g. OpenAI text-embedding-3-small or open-source equivalent)

milestoneFrameworkJson.deliverable_statement: A curated and validated knowledge base of 200,000 historical support interactions from Zendesk and PostgreSQL, prepared for use by the chatbot, with a documented quality score.
A live, deployable chatbot prototype integrated with Zendesk that answers questions with at least 85% measured accuracy against a new benchmark test set, with a full performance report.

A fully operational, production-ready chatbot system that includes: automatic escalation of uncertain answers to a human agent, a dashboard showing real-time accuracy and escalation rates, and a process for continuous logging of all interactions for future improvement.

sdlc_notices: The core risk is achieving and maintaining over 85% accuracy on real customer questions, which requires careful data cleaning and continuous evaluation against a high-quality benchmark.
The system must respond within 3 seconds, requiring optimized retrieval and generation pipelines.
Integration with Zendesk is critical for seamless escalation and requires secure API handling.
Success is not just deployment; it requires a measurable reduction in customer-reported chatbot errors and support escalations.
artifactAJson.business_intent: Revamp the existing support chatbot to dramatically increase answer accuracy from 71% to over 85%, reducing customer frustration and churn. The system will leverage 200,000 historical Zendesk tickets to build a reliable knowledge base and will automatically escalate complex questions to human agents to maintain trust.
